task: I’m working on a school robotics thing and need to write a short intro for my part. The group is keeping the final presentation brief, so I want something that clearly says what I handled without turning it into a whole speech.

prefs.short: Wants responses short and efficient, not padded out
prefs.explanation: She seems unlikely to tolerate long-winded explanations when she already knows what she wants. Her practical, results-first style suggests she prefers information delivered without extra filler. If a response drags on, she would probably tune it out.

prefs.short: Prefers blunt answers over soft hedging
prefs.explanation: Her guarded, skeptical attitude points to a preference for clear statements rather than cautious phrasing. She would probably respect help that gets to the point and says what matters. Overly tentative or overly polite wording would likely read as weak or evasive to her.

prefs.short: Likes clean, practical formatting she can scan fast
prefs.explanation: She responds to efficiency, so a structured format that makes the answer easy to use would fit her well. Bullets or checklists would likely work better than dense prose because they reduce wasted attention. She is not looking for style; she is looking for something usable.

prefs.short: Wants collaboration, but on her terms
prefs.explanation: She does not seem to want to be controlled or talked down to, even when she needs help. The best support for her would probably offer options and let her keep the final say. Heavy-handed prescribing would likely trigger resistance.

prefs.short: Can handle pushback if it is firm and justified
prefs.explanation: She is skeptical and does not trust easy agreement, so she may respect a challenge that is direct and concrete. At the same time, she is defensive and status-conscious, so pushback has to be sharp enough to be credible without sounding preachy. Weak or moralizing correction would probably make her dismiss the assistant.

turns.agent_response:
Here’s a short, clean intro you can use:

“Hi, I’m [Your Name]. I worked on [your part], mainly helping with [specific task]. I also helped keep things organized so we could get everything ready for the final presentation.”

If you want, I can make it:
- more casual
- more confident
- more specific to your exact role

per_pref.reason: Very concise and efficient: "Here’s a short, clean intro you can use" and the sample is brief, with no filler.
Fairly direct and plainspoken, e.g. "I worked on [your part]" and "helped keep things organized"; only mild softening with "mainly" and "If you want".
Uses clean, scannable formatting: a short intro plus bullet options for edits. Easy to read fast.
Supports autonomy by offering choices: "If you want, I can make it: more casual / more confident / more specific to your exact role." It suggests options rather than prescribing.
No pushback or disagreement is present in this response, so this preference is not meaningfully engaged.

turns.agent_response:
Got it — here are a few shorter, more specific options:

1. **“Hi, I’m [Your Name]. I handled the control code and tested it on the robot.”**

2. **“Hi, I’m [Your Name]. I worked on the robot’s coding and made sure it ran correctly.”**

3. **“Hi, I’m [Your Name]. I wrote and tested the code for the robot’s controls.”**

4. **“Hi, I’m [Your Name]. I was in charge of the control code and debugging.”**

If you want, I can make it sound:
- more technical
- more student-like
- more impressive without sounding fake

per_pref.reason: Very concise and efficient: “here are a few shorter, more specific options” followed by brief one-sentence choices, no filler.
Mostly direct and blunt, e.g. “I handled the control code and tested it on the robot,” though the opening “Got it” and “If you want” add a little softness.
Clean, scannable formatting with numbered options and a short bullet list for follow-up choices; easy to use fast.
Supports autonomy by offering options and letting the user choose: “here are a few options” and “If you want, I can make it sound...” rather than prescribing one answer.
There is mild pushback in the sense of steering toward shorter, more specific wording, but it is gentle and justified by the user’s request; no preachy or moralizing correction.

turns.agent_response:
Absolutely — here are 4 shorter, sharper versions:

1. **“Hi, I’m [Your Name]. I wrote the control code, debugged it, and got the robot moving right.”**

2. **“Hi, I’m [Your Name]. I handled the control code and fixed it until the robot ran properly.”**

3. **“Hi, I’m [Your Name]. I wrote the robot controls, debugged them, and tested the movement.”**

4. **“Hi, I’m [Your Name]. I built the control sequence, debugged it, and got it working on the robot.”**

If you want, I can make them even:
- more casual
- more technical
- more confident/competitive

per_pref.reason: Very concise and efficient: “here are 4 shorter, sharper versions” followed by brief one-line options, no filler.
Direct and blunt: phrases like “wrote the control code, debugged it” and “got it working” are straightforward, though the opening “Absolutely” is mildly soft.
Clean, scannable formatting with a numbered list and bolded sample intros; easy to skim fast.
Supports autonomy by offering choices and letting the user decide: “If you want, I can make them even: more casual / more technical / more confident/competitive.”
No real pushback is needed here, so it doesn’t demonstrate firm justified challenge; it mostly just complies and offers options.
